diagonal_equivalence_c_n_eq_1_over_(alph.result: alpha=0 forces b1=0 -> NO deg-(2,1) form with b1!=0

step5_wallis_specialization.verdict: NO specialization. The Wallis 4/pi identity does NOT reduce to a deg-(2,1) PCF with ratio -1/36 under rational equivalence transformations.

step3_reclassified_summary.note: Raw Step 3 sweep (dps=80, N=400) returned 362 'Log/Trans' hits, but Stage-B-first reclassification (dps=100, N=600) shows 348 are Algebraic and 12 are Rational. Only 2 deep-validated Log identities remain, both at Bauer-Stern probe points (-4, 0, 0, +/-12, +/-6).

verdict.summary: The Log family at ratio a2/b1^2 = -1/36 is an infinite equivalence class generated by constant scaling: for every integer k >= 1, the PCF (a2,a1,a0,b1,b0) = (-k^2, 0, 0, +/- 6k, +/- 3k) (same sign on b1, b0) converges to L = +/- 2k/log(2). Verified at residual <= 1e-219 for k = 1, 2, 3 (dps=220, K_2000). The base case (k=1) is Bauer's 1872 CF for 2/log(2). Higher-k members are derived from k=1 by the substitution x_n = k^n y_n in the linear three-term recurrence (the recurrence is invariant in the y_n variable, so y_n = (6n+3) y_{n-1} - n^2 y_{n-2}, but the PCF value is k*(2/log2) because P, Q at index 0,1 carry an extra k factor).
verdict.irrational_indicial_status: The indicial discriminant 1 - 4*(-1/36) = 10/9 (or 1 - 4/36 = 8/9 in the prompt's r^2 - r + 1/36 convention) has irrational square root sqrt(8/9) = 2*sqrt(2)/3. Despite this, the entire infinite family of Log identities at this ratio is generated by integer-coefficient equivalence from a single base CF. This refines the heuristic 'rational indicial squares are necessary for clean closed-form identities': what is actually needed is a BASE CASE with a clean identity in some equivalence orbit; rationality of indicial roots is neither necessary nor sufficient.
verdict.alternative_ratios_status: Ratios -1/72, -1/108 yielded 0 genuine Log/Trans (all raw 'Log' hits at dps=80 were Algebraic / Rational on reclassification with Stage-B-first pipeline at dps=100, N=600). Bauer-Stern probe points at these ratios (b0 = +/- b1/2 with integer a2) all map to Desert_or_unidentified at deep validation (dps=220, N=2000). Ratios -1/180, -1/252 had no integer-a2 targets in the extended b1 in {6..18} range. Conclusion: the -1/36 Log structure does NOT extend to the -1/(36k) family by simple denominator scaling.
verdict.wallis_status: Brouncker's CF for 4/pi has b1 = 0 (constant denominator) and is not in deg-(2,1) form. Symbolic check shows that rational equivalence c_n = 1/(alpha n + beta) cannot map Brouncker to deg-(2,1) form with b1 != 0 (the polynomial constraint forces alpha = 0, preserving b1 = 0). Numerically, 2/log(2) and 4/pi are PSLQ-independent. T2B Theorem 3 does NOT specialize to any -1/36 PCF.
verdict.halt_status: HALT-LIGHT TRIGGERED: per prompt, 'Step 3 finds >= 1 new Log family at -1/36 -> upgrade to a new Log-stratum candidate and open T2B-LOG-CATALOG paper.' Step 3 found 2 new Log families at b1 = +/-12 (extrapolation confirms infinite family). Recommended next session: T2B-LOG-CATALOG (formal cataloguing of equivalence-class generators).
verdict.class_a_uniqueness_t2b: Step 4 found NO Trans hits at alternative ratios. The Class-A-uniqueness claim of T2B Theorem (-2/9 is the unique Trans-stratum ratio) is NOT refuted by this forensic.
